beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and make coffee at home
beats.reasoning: Nathan starts his Friday morning routine at home in Phinney Ridge. Yesterday's energy was moderate (55/100), so a calm, deliberate start fits his pattern of planning around mood and weather.
Commute from Phinney Ridge to Georgetown workspace. Nathan leaves early to settle in before the warm day peaks, consistent with his pattern of arriving early when motivated.

beats.activity: Work on park shade structure designs and sustainable materials
beats.reasoning: Nathan dives into landscape architecture work in the morning when focus is highest. Today's warm forecast (77F high) makes shade structure work particularly relevant and motivating.

beats.activity: Grab lunch and eat at desk while reviewing specs
beats.reasoning: Nathan repeats his pattern from yesterday—quick lunch nearby, then back to the office to eat while working. Keeps momentum on the design project.

beats.activity: Continue design work and wrap up deliverables for the week
beats.reasoning: Afternoon push on designs before heading out. Nathan tends to finish strong on Fridays to clear his mind for weekend plans (camping trip incoming).

beats.activity: Drive home, aware of Capitol Hill Pride road closures on Broadway/Pike
beats.reasoning: Nathan leaves work before evening rush and Pride crowds build. He avoids Broadway/Pike closures by routing through Phinney Ridge, which is his home neighborhood—a natural path.

beats.activity: Meal prep: grill salmon and roast vegetables for the week
beats.reasoning: Nathan values planning and expense tracking, so meal prep on Friday is consistent with his spreadsheet-minded approach. Warm evening makes outdoor grilling appealing.

beats.activity: Relaxation: review fly-fishing catalogs and plan camping gear for weekend trip north; Wind down: read, listen to music, prepare gear for camping
beats.reasoning: Nathan prefers quiet time with close friends over big crowds, so he skips the Pride street fair downtown. Instead, he stays home planning his weekend camping trip—a core passion.
Nathan gets a full 8.5 hours of sleep before his camping trip, ending well before 06:00 on Saturday to allow for an early start to the mountains.

diary: Friday felt like a good exhale. Got through the morning routine without rushing—there's something about a warm day that makes coffee taste better. The drive to the office was smooth; I'd checked the road closures earlier and planned around the Pride festivities, so no surprises there. Spent most of the day buried in shade structure specs and sustainable material sourcing. Grabbed a Molly Moon's salad at my desk, which felt both lazy and productive somehow. By the time I headed home, the city was already buzzing with Pride energy. I caught glimpses of Capitol Hill getting festive as I took the long way around. Made salmon and veggies for the week—something grounding about that ritual. Spent the evening flipping through fly-fishing catalogs like a kid, planning out gear for a camping trip north this weekend. The whole day had this underlying current of anticipation, like the season shifting into something good.
thought_process: I was aware of Pride happening tonight but chose to stay home rather than head downtown. Not about the event itself—I support it—but I'm genuinely excited about this camping trip and wanted headspace to plan it properly. Work felt purposeful today; I'm getting closer to finalizing these park designs, which matters to me. The warm weather helped my mood too. I think I'm at that point where I need less city stimulation and more time in nature. The salmon prep was deliberate—grounding myself, setting up the week ahead. Everything felt intentional today, less reactive.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
